answer:
Yes, Jira's free plan includes full access to the Jira REST API [1][2]. You can use the API for integrations, scripts, or custom apps on a self-serve basis [3].

To authenticate your API calls, generate an API token through your Atlassian account settings [1]. This token serves as your credentials for requests directed at your Jira cloud instance (e.g., `https://your-domain.atlassian.net/rest/api/3/issue`) [3][1]. You can find documentation and specific endpoint examples on the Atlassian developer platform [4]. No special developer license is required to use the REST API on a standard free Jira cloud instance [5][3][1].
